---
title: "Research"
author: "Nico Steckhan"
output: html_document
---

My research sits between translational medicine, data science, and connected health. I work on methods that make complex, real-world health data usable for explanation, prediction, and practical decision support.

## Current directions

- Multimodal sensor data and digital phenotyping
- Wearables and continuous glucose monitoring in everyday settings
- Machine learning and sensor fusion for health-related behavior and physiology
- AI-supported systems for occupational and health research

## Previous and ongoing topics

- Fasting, metabolism, and compliance assessment in remote studies
- Chronotype, glucose dynamics, and behavioral health signals
- Bioinformatics and high-dimensional phenotype prediction
- Placebo effects, stress physiology, and psychobiological responses
- Digital health infrastructure and reproducible research workflows

## Selected publications

- Steckhan, N. (2025). [Sensor-based evaluation of intermittent fasting regimes: a machine learning and statistical approach](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41366-025-01889-0).
- Steckhan, N. (2025). [Brave New Komplementaer- und Alternativmedizin (CAM)?](https://doi.org/10.1055/a-2500-3348).
- Steckhan, N. et al. (2023). [Fasting state prediction using continuous glucose monitoring, activity and nutritional data: a tool for compliance evaluation in remote fasting trials](https://doi.org/10.1055/s-0043-1767911).
- Hecker, P., Steckhan, N. et al. (2022). [Voice Analysis for Neurological Disorder Recognition: A Systematic Review and Perspective on Emerging Trends](https://doi.org/10.3389/fdgth.2022.842301).
- Kessler, C. S. et al. (2018). [A nonrandomized controlled clinical pilot trial on 8 wk of intermittent fasting (24 h/wk)](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.nut.2017.08.004).

A fuller list of publications is available in my [CV](files/CV.pdf). You can also browse publications on [ResearchGate](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Nico-Steckhan) and [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=search_authors&mauthors=Nico+Steckhan&hl=en).

## Collaboration

I enjoy collaborations that combine clinical questions, sensing technologies, and rigorous quantitative methods. If you are working on digital phenotyping, wearable sensing, or machine learning for real-world health data, feel free to get in touch.
